description:
Visualize the 3D domain architecture of yeast phenylalanine tRNA using PyMOL. 

Starting from the structure 1EHZ.pdb (chain A only), perform the following:
1. Color-code the four main structural domains using distinct colors: Acceptor stem (residues 1-7, 66-72), D-arm (residues 10-25), Anticodon arm (residues 27-43), and T-arm / TΨC arm (residues 49-65).
2. Measure the straight-line distance (in Angstroms) from the 3' terminal nucleotide (residue 76) to the middle anticodon nucleotide (residue 35).
3. Identify at least one post-transcriptionally modified (non-standard) nucleotide residue in this structure by its 3-letter PDB code or full name.
4. Render and save a high-quality visualization to '~/PyMOL_Data/images/trna_domains.png'.
5. Write a structural analysis report to '~/PyMOL_Data/trna_structure_report.txt'. The report must be at least 5 lines long and contain: the names of the 4 domains, the measured end-to-end distance, and the modified nucleotide(s) you identified.
